sentiment.rationale: Daniel and his companions are blessed by God for their faithfulness in abstaining from the king's food, showing divine favor and wisdom.
God reveals Nebuchadnezzar's dream to Daniel, who declares God's power to set up and remove kings; the stone cut without hands destroys all earthly kingdoms.
Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego refuse to worship the golden image and are delivered from the fiery furnace by divine intervention.
Nebuchadnezzar is humbled by God, driven to live as a beast until he acknowledges divine sovereignty; he repents and is restored.
Belshazzar desecrates the temple vessels and the handwriting on the wall pronounces divine judgment; that night Babylon falls.
Daniel is cast into the lions' den for praying to God but is miraculously preserved; Darius decrees honor to the God of Daniel.
Daniel's vision of four beasts and the Ancient of Days culminates in the Son of Man receiving an everlasting dominion and kingdom.
The vision of the ram and he-goat foretells the rise and fall of empires and the desolation of the sanctuary.
Daniel's profound prayer of confession and supplication for Israel's sins, followed by Gabriel's revelation of the seventy weeks prophecy.
Daniel mourns and fasts for three weeks before receiving a heavenly vision; the messenger describes spiritual warfare among angelic princes.
A detailed prophecy of wars between the kings of the north and south, full of conflict, treachery, and persecution of the saints.
The promise of resurrection, Michael standing up for Israel, and the assurance that Daniel shall stand in his lot at the end of days.

missingCharacters.name: Arioch
missingCharacters.description: Captain of Nebuchadnezzar's guard who was commanded to destroy the wise men of Babylon; he brought Daniel before the king to interpret the dream.

missingCharacters.name: Belshazzar
missingCharacters.description: Last king of Babylon who saw the handwriting on the wall at his feast; slain the night Babylon fell to the Medes and Persians.

missingCharacters.name: Abed-nego
missingCharacters.description: One of Daniel's three companions (also called Azariah) who refused to worship Nebuchadnezzar's golden image and was delivered from the fiery furnace.

summaries.summary: Daniel and three companions are taken captive to Babylon; they refuse the king's food, are blessed with wisdom, and excel above all the magicians.
Nebuchadnezzar dreams of a great image; Daniel receives the interpretation from God, revealing successive world kingdoms and an everlasting divine kingdom.
Nebuchadnezzar erects a golden image; Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego refuse to worship it and are cast into a fiery furnace but delivered by God.
Nebuchadnezzar recounts his dream of a great tree; Daniel interprets it as the king's coming humiliation, which is fulfilled until he acknowledges God's sovereignty.
At Belshazzar's feast, a hand writes on the wall; Daniel interprets the message as divine judgment, and that night Belshazzar is slain and Darius takes the kingdom.
Daniel's rivals conspire to have him thrown into the lions' den for praying to God; he is miraculously preserved, and Darius decrees reverence for Daniel's God.
Daniel sees a vision of four great beasts, the Ancient of Days on his throne, and one like the Son of Man receiving an everlasting dominion.
Daniel sees the vision of the ram and the he-goat, symbolizing the Medo-Persian and Greek empires; Gabriel explains the vision and its timing.
Daniel prays a confession for Israel's sins; Gabriel appears and reveals the prophecy of the seventy weeks concerning the coming of the Messiah.
Daniel fasts and mourns for three weeks; a glorious heavenly messenger appears and describes the spiritual warfare delaying his arrival.
A detailed prophecy of wars between the kings of the north and south, the desecration of the sanctuary, and the persecution of the faithful.
Michael shall stand up for Israel; the dead shall be resurrected; Daniel is told to seal the book and is promised he shall stand in his lot at the end of days.

crossReferences.note: The kingdom of God shall break in pieces all other kingdoms and stand forever.
The stone cut out of the mountain without hands rolling forth to fill the whole earth.
When thou walkest through the fire, thou shalt not be burned.
The Son of Man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
Dominion and glory and a kingdom given to the Lamb.
Seventy weeks determined to finish the transgression and bring in everlasting righteousness.
Many that sleep in the dust shall awake, some to everlasting life and some to shame.

verses.reason: Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the king's meat.
Daniel's hymn of praise declaring God changes times and seasons, removes and sets up kings.
The prophecy of God's kingdom that shall never be destroyed—the stone cut without hands.
The bold declaration of faith: our God is able to deliver us from the fiery furnace.
Nebuchadnezzar sees a fourth figure in the furnace like the Son of God.
Nebuchadnezzar declares God's kingdom is everlasting and his dominion from generation to generation.
TEKEL: Thou art weighed in the balances, and art found wanting.
Daniel prayed three times a day as was his custom, despite the king's decree.
God sent his angel and shut the lions' mouths.
The vision of one like the Son of man coming with the clouds of heaven to the Ancient of days.
He was given dominion, glory, and a kingdom that all peoples and nations should serve him.
To the Lord our God belong mercies and forgivenesses, though we have rebelled against him.
The seventy weeks prophecy pointing to the Messiah.
Many that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake—a key Old Testament resurrection text.
They that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament.
